asset_type: TaraHome blog article cover, wide landscape editorial photograph
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome blog article cover, wide landscape editorial photograph
Primary request: Show a real home's local smart-lighting routine continuing to work during an internet outage, expressed only through a believable residential moment rather than a diagram.
Scene/backdrop: Warm contemporary entry hall opening into a living room at early evening, natural oak console and flooring, soft plaster walls, linen and ceramic details, restrained dark-green accents.
Subject: A homeowner has just entered and a practical ceiling or wall light has automatically illuminated in response to a small ordinary motion/contact sensor near the doorway. On the console in the middle distance, a normal unbranded home internet router has a subtle red status light, while a small unbranded smart-home hub remains powered; the human moment and illuminated room are the focus.
Style/medium: Clean high-end but candid editorial interior photography, photorealistic, authentic household textures, not glossy CGI.
Composition/framing: Wide 16:9 horizontal composition suitable for both a full article hero and a tighter blog-card crop; keep the main action and key objects within the central 70 percent; natural eye-level perspective; no headline space requirement.
Lighting/mood: Warm practical lamps against cool dusk window light, calm, reassuring, lived-in, realistic exposure.
Color palette: Warm wood, cream, muted charcoal, restrained dark forest green.
Materials/textures: Visible oak grain, woven fabric, matte ceramics, realistic painted walls, everyday imperfection.
Constraints: No text, no letters, no logos, no brand marks, no watermark. Devices must look like ordinary real consumer hardware, not futuristic or fantastical. No phone screen or app interface.
Avoid: embedded headline or title poster; fake charts or graphs; decorative routing, network, path, signal, or glowing connection-line overlays; translucent panels; inset frames; floating UI cards; progress bars; generic AI diagram look; repeated beige device diagram; exaggerated sci-fi lighting; fake branded SmartThings or Samsung hardware.
